title: Choose streams vs pub/sub by durability need
description: Use Streams when messages must be retained, acked, and replayable; use pub/sub only for fire-and-forget fan-out.
body: Use when sending messages through Redis. Do not use pub/sub for work that must not be lost — pub/sub delivers only to currently-connected subscribers and drops messages for anyone offline. Actions: use Redis Streams with consumer groups when you need persistence, per-consumer acknowledgements (XACK), redelivery of unacked entries (XPENDING/XCLAIM), and replay from an offset; reserve pub/sub for ephemeral notifications where loss is acceptable. Validation signals include a test that a stream consumer that was offline still receives backlogged entries and that unacked entries can be reclaimed. Risk boundary: using pub/sub for at-least-once work silently drops messages whenever a consumer is down — a correctness finding requiring streams or an external broker.
